preset_description: Dark = decision signal. Emphasis accumulates toward conversion — trust anchors and commitment moments are dark, top-of-funnel is light.

brief.audience: Saas founders seeking an AI-ready design system

accent_tokens._note: Brand primary CTA color. Global — not per-preset. Two variants: button on a light/neutral section vs. on a dark section.

beats.name: Orientation
beats.rationale: Immediately establishes context for SaaS founders: what MODE is (an AI-ready design system), who it's for, and why it matters now. Grounds the Validator archetype with a clear, no-hype framing before any claims are made.

beats.name: Credibility
beats.rationale: Validators need trust signals early to continue reading. Placing social proof—customer logos, founder testimonials, notable adopters—right after orientation satisfies their instinct to verify legitimacy before investing attention in feature details.

beats.name: Value
beats.rationale: With credibility established, we can now unpack MODE's capabilities: intent-aware components, AI-readiness, design-to-code workflow. Framed as outcome promises relevant to SaaS founders (ship faster, maintain consistency, future-proof the stack).

beats.name: Evidence
beats.rationale: Critical for the Validator archetype. Concrete metrics, case study snippets, and before/after data points substantiate the value claims just made. This beat converts skepticism into confidence—proof over promise.

beats.name: Conversion
beats.rationale: Awareness-stage soft CTA: invite to explore a free trial, join a waitlist, or access a starter kit—low commitment, high curiosity. No pricing or plan comparison; the ask matches the early funnel position while capitalizing on the proof-driven momentum built for Validators.

beats.name: Recovery
beats.rationale: Provides secondary engagement paths for those not ready to convert: documentation links, community access, newsletter signup, and footer navigation. Ensures no visitor leaves without a next step, capturing residual intent from awareness-stage traffic.

sections.name: Navigation
sections.rationale: Global navigation — wayfinding and persistent CTA for the session.

sections.name: Hero
sections.rationale: Immediately establishes context for SaaS founders: what MODE is (an AI-ready design system), who it's for, and why it matters now. Grounds the Validator archetype with a clear, no-hype framing before any claims are made.

sections.name: Social proof
sections.rationale: Validators need trust signals early to continue reading. Placing social proof—customer logos, founder testimonials, notable adopters—right after orientation satisfies their instinct to verify legitimacy before investing attention in feature details.

sections.name: Footer
sections.rationale: Page close — brand mark, minimal navigation, and legal.

page.reasoning: Awareness stage funnel requires conversion focus. Notes specify: 'minimal variant for conversion-focused pages — strip nav_links, keep logo and single CTA only.' The persistent CTA in nav is critical for top-funnel conversion.

Validator archetype demands trust signals. Notes confirm: 'Validator variant always includes trust_signal' and 'with-social-proof variant places LogoStrip immediately below CTACluster,' directly addressing validator needs in awareness stage.

slots.headline: Your design system knows how to render. MODE teaches it why.
slots.subhead: MODE is the intelligence layer for code-first SaaS design systems — resolving component behavior, visual emphasis, and content register to the right experience for every archetype, funnel stage, and context. No manual variants. No QA overhead.
slots.trust_signal: One-time payment. Lifetime access. Lives in your codebase, not a vendor dashboard.

page.reasoning: Validator archetype requires social proof leadership. Notes state: 'Validator variant leads with quote, logos below,' which builds credibility essential for converting skeptical SaaS founders at awareness stage.

slots.headline: Adopted by founders who build in code and ship without handoff cycles.
slots.quote: We replaced three weeks of variant QA with a single brief. MODE made the right call on archetype and funnel stage before we touched a component.
slots.attribution: Early access founder, B2B SaaS — 2024 customer survey

page.reasoning: Conversion goal requires footer recovery mechanism. Notes specify: 'with-cta variant recaptures departing users on key conversion pages,' critical for capturing validators moving toward paid decision.
